# Polaris Documentation

> User and developer documentation for Polaris Finance — the self-scaling stablecoin operating system. Mint pAssets backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills. No CEXs. No compromises.

## Overview

- [Polaris Finance Documentation](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/): Polaris is the pETH-powered yield layer for all of DeFi, minting pAssets backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills. No CEXs. No compromises.
- [Changelog](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/changelog): A documentation changelog for the Polaris docs, in Keep a Changelog style. This tracks changes to the docs only, not protocol release history.
- [Polaris Core Concepts](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/core-concepts): A one-page map of the Polaris system, with every key term defined briefly and linked to its full explanation.
- [Getting Started with Polaris](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/getting-started): What you need to use Polaris and the basic flow: deposit ETH, receive pETH, open a trove and mint a pAsset, and optionally earn yield in the Stability Pool.
- [Polaris Launch Status](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/launch-status): The canonical status page for Polaris launch phase, official networks, app URL, contract addresses, audit status, and what is actionable today.
- [Why Polaris Finance](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/why-polaris): The stablecoin trilemma, the degradation pattern, and how Polaris is architected so that trustlessness and scale stop being mutually exclusive.

## Explainers

- [Polaris Timed Explainers](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/explainers): Short, medium, and complete Polaris explainers that introduce pETH, pAssets, POLAR, and the protocol flywheel.
- [Polaris 10-Minute Introduction](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/explainers/10-min): The complete Polaris system from first principles — the problem, the mechanics, and the flywheel.
- [Polaris 2-Minute Overview](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/explainers/2-min): What Polaris is, in one sitting.
- [Polaris 5-Minute Presentation](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/explainers/5-min): The three engines and the flywheel — a complete picture of Polaris.

## Choose Your Path

- [Polaris User Paths](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths): Role-based Polaris reading paths for borrowers, yield users, pETH holders, POLAR participants, developers, and safety verification.
- [Borrow Polaris pAssets](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths/borrow-passets): The borrower path for using Polaris: get pETH, open a trove, mint a pAsset, manage collateralization, and verify launch status before interacting.
- [Convert and Lock POLAR on Polaris](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths/convert-lock-polar): The POLAR path for users who want to understand pETH-to-POLAR conversion, vePOLAR locking, bounded stewardship, and launch status.
- [Develop on Polaris](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths/develop): The developer path for integrating with Polaris before launch artifacts are published.
- [Earn Yield on Polaris](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths/earn-yield): The yield path for Polaris users: Stability Pool deposits, pETH yield sources, Fee Router distribution, and launch-time verification.
- [Hold or Use Polaris pETH](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths/hold-use-peth): The pETH path for users who want exposure to the bonding curve, a rising floor, external collateral use, or Polaris trove collateral.
- [Polaris Safety and Verification](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/paths/safety-verification): The safety path for verifying Polaris launch status, official contracts, audit status, intended invariants, and user risk before interacting.

## Minting & Troves

- [Polaris Minting and Troves](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/minting): The Polaris minting hub for pAssets, troves, collateralization, pAsset catalog entries, and borrower risk.
- [Managing a Polaris Trove](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/minting/managing-your-trove): Manage a Polaris trove around its Individual Collateralization Ratio (ICR) — add collateral, mint or repay debt, track interest, and close out cleanly.
- [Minting Polaris pAssets](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/minting/minting-passets): How the Polaris CDP model mints pAssets directly from the protocol against pETH collateral, with autonomous market-aware interest rates.
- [Open a Polaris Trove](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/minting/open-a-trove): Step-by-step guide to opening a Polaris trove — deposit pETH, mint a pAsset, and pick a safe buffer to stay clear of liquidation and redemption.
- [Polaris Asset Catalog](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/minting/passet-catalog): The pAsset debt tokens Polaris can mint (pUSD, pGOLD, pCHF, pBigMac), how pETH differs as collateral, and how StablecoinOS can add new assets.

## pETH & Bonding Curve

- [Polaris pETH and Bonding Curve](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/peth): The pETH hub for the bonding curve, floor price, ETH-denominated downside, exit assumptions, and pETH collateral use.
- [pETH Bonding Curve](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/peth/bonding-curve): How one two-way bonding curve gives pETH contract-native liquidity, a rising floor price, and protocol-wide value capture, with no liquidity providers required.
- [pETH Floor Price](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/peth/floor-price): pETH's floor price is a mathematically enforced minimum under the bonding-curve invariants. What it is, why it rises, and how the floor ratio frames downside.

## Earning Yield

- [Polaris Yield](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/yield): The Polaris yield hub for Stability Pool deposits, pETH and pAsset yield sources, Fee Router distribution, and launch status.
- [Deposit to the Polaris Stability Pool](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/yield/deposit-to-stability-pool): Step-by-step instructions to deposit a pAsset into the Stability Pool, claim rewards, and withdraw.
- [Polaris Stability Pool](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/yield/stability-pool): The Stability Pool is a reserve of pAssets that backstops liquidations and is the primary venue for earning yield on Polaris.
- [Polaris Yield Sources](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/yield/yield-sources): Every source of pAsset yield on Polaris is onchain, internal to the protocol, and scales with activity — yield that is owned, not rented.

## Redemptions & Liquidations

- [Polaris Redemptions and Liquidations](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/redemptions-liquidations): The Polaris risk-mechanics hub for pAsset redemptions, liquidations, Recovery Mode, borrower impact, and Stability Pool exposure.
- [Polaris Liquidations](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations): A trove below its minimum collateralization ratio is liquidated to keep the pAsset backed. The Stability Pool absorbs the debt, or it redistributes to troves.
- [Polaris Recovery Mode](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode): Recovery Mode is the protective state Polaris enters when system reserve backing runs thin relative to total debt. How it works, and how to stay safe.
- [pAsset Redemptions](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/redemptions-liquidations/redemptions): A redemption swaps a below-peg pAsset for its pETH collateral at face value minus a fee. How the arbitrage restores peg, and what it means for borrowers.

## POLAR & Conversion

- [Polaris POLAR and Conversion](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/polar): The POLAR hub for the stewardship token, tokenomics, pETH-to-POLAR conversion auctions, and user conversion flow.
- [POLAR Conversion Auctions](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/polar/conversion-auctions): The conversion auction bridges pETH and POLAR — a spike-and-decay auction where burning pETH mints POLAR, raises the floor price, and releases ETH yield.
- [Participate in a POLAR Conversion](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/polar/participate-in-conversion): Step-by-step guide to executing a conversion: watch the decaying price, decide when it beats POLAR's market price, then burn pETH for POLAR and any ETH rebate.
- [POLAR Token](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/polar/polar-token): Why POLAR exists, how it is minted only by burning pETH, and why its dilution is productive — every unit improves the protocol's fundamentals.
- [POLAR Tokenomics](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/polar/tokenomics): How POLAR's economics work: issuance only by burning pETH, vePOLAR vote-escrow, Fee Router yield, and the Polar Council. No gauges, bribes, or emissions.

## Stewardship

- [Polaris Stewardship](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/stewardship): The Polaris stewardship hub for vePOLAR, Fee Router distribution, Flows, bounded decisions, and operator prerequisites.
- [Polaris Fee Router](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/stewardship/fee-router): Polaris's yield plumbing: the Fee Router buffers protocol revenue and routes pETH yield to troves, POLAR stakers, and Flow recipients under bounded rules.
- [Polaris Flows](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/stewardship/flows): Flows distribute protocol revenue to contracts building on Polaris: hard-asset revenue sharing by formula, with no gauges, no bribes, and no emissions.
- [Polaris Stewardship, not Governance](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/stewardship/stewardship): Polaris keeps its core immutable and lets vePOLAR steward only bounded parameters, onboarding decisions, and edge processes that cannot be automated.
- [vePOLAR Locking and Stewardship](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/stewardship/vepolar): vePOLAR is vote-escrowed POLAR. Lock POLAR to receive stewardship rights and a share of protocol yield without touching the immutable core.

## Resources

- [Polaris Resources](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources): Reference pages for Polaris terminology, risk disclosure, FAQ, contracts, audits, security, brand assets, and launch verification.
- [Polaris Audits & Security](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources/audits-security): Polaris security model, audit-report status, bug-bounty status, and disclosure guidance. Pre-launch reports and bounty details are not published yet.
- [Polaris Brand Assets](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources/brand-assets): Polaris logos, the Celestial Night Sky palette, and typography, with usage do's and don'ts. Dark mode is the canonical surface.
- [Polaris Contracts & Addresses](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources/contracts): The Polaris contract set: a shared core plus one instance per pAsset. Addresses publish at deployment; verify network, chain ID, and source first.
- [Polaris Finance FAQ](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources/faq): Frequently asked questions about Polaris: what it is, how minting and troves work, how the peg holds, where yield comes from, and how security works.
- [Polaris Glossary](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources/glossary): Every Polaris term defined in one or two sentences, alphabetized and linked to its full explanation. The fast way to look anything up.
- [Polaris Risk Disclosure](https://docs.polarisfinance.io/resources/risk-disclosure): A comprehensive catalog of the risks of using Polaris, each paired with the mechanism that mitigates it, without implying any risk is eliminated.
